question.text: Which characteristic is the best example of an inherited trait?

prediction.reasoning: Strong retrieved evidence selected this answer.

metadata.retrieval_query: Which characteristic is the best example of an inherited trait?
metadata.evidence_preview:
[1] Heredity - Wikipedia
URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heredity
. The study of heredity in biology is genetics. Overview [edit]In humans, eye color is an example of an inherited characteristic: an individual might inherit the "brown-eye trait" from one of the parents.[1] Inherited traits are controlled by genes and the complete set of genes within an organism's genome is called its genotype.[2] The complete set of observable traits of the structure and behavior of an organism is called its phenotype

[2] 50 Inherited Traits Examples (2026) - Helpful Professor
URL: https://helpfulprofessor.com/inherited-traits-examples/
. Inherited Traits Examples - Eye color - Hair color - Hair texture (straight, wavy, curly) - Skin color - Freckles or no freckles - Cleft chin - Earlobe attachment (attached or free-hanging) - Height - Natural pitch of voice - Widow’s peak - Dimples - Nose shape - Finger length - Toe length - Handedness (right or left-handedness) - Eye shape - Presence or absence of male pattern baldness - Tendency toward obesity - Lactose intolerance - Ability

question.text: Most of the bacteria in a forest ecosystem are best classified as which of the following types of organisms?

metadata.retrieval_query: Most of the bacteria in a forest ecosystem are best classified as which of the following types of organisms?
metadata.evidence_preview:
[1] How Are Bacteria Classified In A Forest Ecosystem?
URL: https://ecologiclife.com/how-are-bacteria-classified-in-a-forest-ecosystem.html
Bacteria and fungi in a forest ecosystem are generally classified as decomposers, producers, secondary consumers, primary consumers, and tertiary consumers. If plants, algae, and autotrophic bacteria vanished from the Earth, animals, fungi, and other heterotrophs would soon disappear.

[2] How Are Bacteria Classified In A Forest Ecosystem? - MAWEB
URL: https://maweb.org/how-are-bacteria-classified-in-a-forest-ecosystem/
. Conclusion: The Significance of Bacterial Classification Understanding how bacteria are classified in forest ecosystems is critical for appreciating their immense contributions to health and sustainability. From nutrient cycling and decomposition to their complex interactions with plants and fungi, these microorganisms play vital roles that often go unnoticed
